stem: A pupil is comparing the AND, OR and NOT logic gates.
model_answer: AND outputs 1 only when ALL inputs are 1, otherwise it outputs 0. OR outputs 1 when AT LEAST ONE input is 1; it only outputs 0 when every input is 0. NOT (the inverter) takes a single input and outputs the opposite — 0 becomes 1 and 1 becomes 0. The truth tables capture this exhaustively for two-input AND and OR (4 rows each) and one-input NOT (2 rows).
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award one mark per correct gate description; partial credit for getting two right.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe what AND, OR and NOT gates do.

mark_points.text: AND outputs 1 only when all inputs are 1
OR outputs 1 when at least one input is 1 (only 0 when all inputs are 0)
NOT outputs the opposite of its single input (1↔0)
